summary: Round-2 battery-backstop implementation (plan 02): phases 1, 2, 3, 5 complete, build-verified, committed. (1) battery-suspend-backstop root service+timer in modules/system/power.nix - polls /sys/class/power_supply/BAT* every 60s, systemctl suspend -i at <=10% while Discharging, inhibitor-proof, intentional re-suspend loop documented in banner (commit c72c4cf). (2) GNOME sleep-inactive-battery-timeout 900->3600 with explicit sleep-inactive-battery-type=suspend (commit 2386332). (3) Claude SessionStart inhibitor payload swapped to tail --pid=$PPID -f /dev/null tether plus stale-pidfile reap; SessionEnd unchanged; hooks remain SESSION-scoped per user decision 1 (commit 29fdd1f). (5) docs/gnome-settings.md: battery backstop bullet, 60-min caveat, accepted open-Claude-session limitation stated plainly, all 15-minute-backstop claims removed (commit 09eb586). Both plan-flagged empirical dependencies VERIFIED before shipping: hook-shell $PPID is the owning claude process (deployed-settings probe + scratch session, deployed file restored from backup), and the systemd-inhibit tail-tether releases within ~1s of owner kill -9 (isolated live test). Backstop branch logic exercised against fake sysfs values (fires at 9/10 Discharging, silent at 11/Not charging/Charging; peripheral hid-* battery excluded by BAT* glob); store-script dry-run silent on AC/100%. All builds green: nix flake check + nixos-rebuild build .#hamsa after every config phase (final closure nixos-system-hamsa-26.05.20260710.8f0500b). Phase 4 [BLOCKED] pending user: interactive sudo unavailable; no switch, no suspend, no battery manipulation performed.
blockers: Phase 4 activation is user-gated: sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#hamsa + reboot, then the runtime checklist in summaries/02_battery-level-backstop-summary.md (backstop timer live, safe suspend vs suspend -i A/B test under held inhibitors, live gsettings, HandleLidSwitch=lock, scratch-session kill -9 tether check)

artifacts.summary: battery-suspend-backstop service + timer (BAT* glob, threshold 10, suspend -i, OnBootSec=2min/OnUnitActiveSec=60s)
battery idle-suspend 3600s + explicit suspend type with inhibitor-caveat comments
SessionStart inhibitor tethered to owning claude process ($PPID verified) + stale-pidfile reap
10% backstop documented, 60-min battery idle-suspend caveat, accepted limitation, stale 15-minute claims removed
Round-2 implementation summary incl. USER ACTIVATION CHECKLIST and one-time stale-pidfile cleanup command

continuation_context: All round-2 config work is committed and build-green; nothing further is agent-actionable until the user activates. Fixed decisions honored: hooks SESSION-scoped (no UserPromptSubmit/Stop), requirement (B) accepted as partial (open-idle Claude terminal blocks idle-suspend until the 10% backstop), services.upower.* untouched. After user activation + reboot, the remaining Phase 4 items are: timer listed and ticking, suspend/suspend -i A/B test (USER), live gsettings 3600/suspend/nothing, HandleLidSwitch lock (+ physical lid check, USER), deployed-hooks diff empty, scratch-session kill -9 releases inhibitor. Any failed check loops back into the owning phase's file; task stays partial until Phase 4 passes.
next_steps: User runs: sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#hamsa, reboot, then the Phase 4 checklist in summaries/02_battery-level-backstop-summary.md; on all-green, mark Phase 4 complete and the task completed.
